description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and a flanged base. The hydrant features two large side outlets and a smaller front outlet, all capped, with the front outlet protruding more prominently. The surface is painted a weathered red, showing signs of wear, chipping, and some rust, giving it an authentic, utilitarian appearance. The top of the hydrant has a central operating nut, used for opening the valve to release water. The base is reinforced with a series of vertical ridges and bolts, suggesting robust construction. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant is shown isolated without any curb or sidewalk context.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with a domed top, two side outlets, and a prominent front outlet, featuring a rugged, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central outlet and operating nut, with both side outlets partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the left outlet and the cylindrical body, with the front outlet visible in profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying both side outlets symmetrically, with the front outlet not visible.
description_view_3: Side view showing the right outlet and the cylindrical body, with the front outlet visible in profile.
